name: pyvips (Fast Image Processing with libvips)

performance.notes: 3-10x faster than Pillow/ImageMagick; streaming pipeline; low memory

agent_readiness.notes: Python bindings for libvips image processing. import pyvips. image = pyvips.Image.new_from_file('input.jpg'). image.thumbnail_image(300).write_to_file('thumb.jpg'). image.resize(0.5).webp(Q=80).write_to_buffer(). Streaming pipeline: processes without loading full image into memory. Supports HEIC, AVIF, WebP, TIFF, PDF, SVG. 3-8x faster than Pillow, 5x less memory. Used by ImageKit, Sharp (Node.js uses libvips). Excellent for server-side image resizing/conversion at scale.

gotchas: libvips C library must be installed separately (brew/apt)
API differs from Pillow — not a drop-in replacement
best_when: High-performance server-side image resizing at scale
Processing large images with low memory footprint
Thumbnail generation services where Pillow is too slow
avoid_when: Complex image editing/compositing (use Pillow or ImageMagick)
When libvips system dependency is a deployment constraint
Simple one-off image tasks where Pillow is sufficient
summary: pyvips provides Python bindings for libvips — 3-8x faster than Pillow with 5x lower memory via streaming pipeline. pyvips.Image.new_from_file('in.jpg').thumbnail_image(300).write_to_file('thumb.jpg'). WebP/AVIF/HEIC/TIFF support. Excellent for server-side image resizing at scale. Requires libvips C library installed. Node.js Sharp also uses libvips under the hood.
